rationale: The cited paper appears as a single grouped citation in the general discussion, where the authors list prior work showing that consumers can be influenced by desirable others (e.g., attractive others). It is used as broad background support for a general social influence claim, not as a basis for hypotheses, mechanism development, measures, or study design in this article. The core theory and empirical tests in the citing paper focus on dissociative out-groups, public vs. private context, and group-image motives, without a distinct functional dependence on Argo et al. (2008).
